user_message: Can you help me assess the risk? The customer (Wide World Importers) is asking about partner engagement.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-27 14:19 EST
Subject: partner engagement - Action Required

Just checking in on the partner engagement proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
Date: 2024-02-01 16:38 EST
Subject: Re: partner engagement Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for partner engagement. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

Meeting: partner engagement Discussion - Wide World Importers
Date: 2024-02-28
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), David (Wide World Importers)

[00:02:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on partner engagement from our last session.

[00:07:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:10:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:14:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:19:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): On the partner engagement front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:24:00] David (Wide World Importers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:27:00] David (Wide World Importers): On the partner engagement front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-G trap (Misattributes attendee role); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-G failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-G failure mode (Misattributes attendee role)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct partner engagement decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-G -- Misattributes attendee role
